label: 1744 CE
era_summary: 1744 (MDCCXLIV) was a leap year starting on Wednesday of the Gregorian calendar and a leap year starting on Sunday of the Julian calendar, the 1744th year of the Common Era (CE) and Anno Domini (AD) designations, the 744th year of the 2nd millennium, the 44th year of the 18th century, and the 5th year of the 1740s decade. As of the start of 1744, the Gregorian calendar was 11 days ahead of the Julian calendar, which remained in localized use until 1923.

rome_italy.summary: Brecke records no major conflicts touching this region in 1744 (safety scored at the era baseline of 85). No Maddison GDP coverage for this region — economy held at neutral 50, reflecting the thin pre-modern record outside the European core. Governance held at neutral (V-Dem coverage starts 1789).

andalusia_iberia.summary: Active conflicts in 1744: Britain, Austria, United Provinces-Spain, Prussia, France, Bavaria, 1740-48; Matamba-Portugal (Ngola, Angola), 1744; Russia-Chukchis (eastern Siberia), 1744-47. Maddison records GDP/capita 1972 (PRT, 1744) — used as the economy proxy for the whole region. Governance held at neutral (V-Dem coverage starts 1789).
